test_cases.test_case: Successful Hotels search redirects to Hotels results listing
preconditions: User is a Visitor (not authenticated)
User is on the Home page with the Hotels tab selected and ready to provide search inputs
steps: 1. Enter <destination> in the Destination field on the Hotels tab
2. Enter <check-in date> in the Check In field
3. Enter <check-out date> in the Check Out field
4. Enter <number of rooms> in the Number Of Rooms field
5. Enter <adults> in the Adults field and <children> in the Children field
6. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: The Hotels Search & Listing page opens; a results list is visible and the search summary on the page shows the entered <destination> and the selected <check-in>–<check-out> dates

test_cases.test_case: Successful Flights search (select trip type and cabin) redirects to Flights results listing
preconditions: User is a Visitor (not authenticated)
User is on the Home page with the Flights tab selected and ready to provide search inputs
steps: 1. Select <trip type> from the Trip Type dropdown on the Flights tab
2. Enter <departure city> in the Departure City field
3. Enter <arrival city> in the Arrival City field
4. Enter <travel dates> in the Travel Dates field (or appropriate date fields for the selected trip type)
5. Enter <adults> in the Adults field and optionally <children> and <infants>
6. Select <cabin class> from the Cabin Class dropdown
7. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: The Flights Search & Listing page opens; flight results are displayed and the page shows a search summary with <departure city> to <arrival city>, the selected <travel dates>, passenger counts, and the chosen <cabin class>

test_cases.test_case: Successful Tours search redirects to Tours results listing
preconditions: User is a Visitor (not authenticated)
User is on the Home page with the Tours tab selected and ready to provide search inputs
steps: 1. Enter <destination> in the Destination field on the Tours tab
2. Enter <travel date range> in the Travel Date Range field
3. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: The Tours Search & Listing page opens; tour results are visible and the search summary shows the entered <destination> and the selected <travel date range>

test_cases.test_case: Successful Cars search redirects to Cars results listing
preconditions: User is a Visitor (not authenticated)
User is on the Home page with the Cars tab selected and ready to provide search inputs
steps: 1. Enter <pick-up location> in the Pick Up Location field on the Cars tab
2. Enter <drop-off location> in the Drop Off Location field
3. Enter <pick-up date and time> in the Pick Up Date Time field
4. Enter <drop-off date and time> in the Drop Off Date Time field
5. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: The Cars Search & Listing page opens; vehicle results are displayed and the page shows a search summary with the entered <pick-up location> to <drop-off location> and the selected pick-up and drop-off date/times

test_cases.test_case: Hotels: Destination left blank (required field)

4. Enter <valid check-out date> in the Check_Out field

6. Enter <valid number> in Adults
7. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit and no redirection occurs; an inline validation error appears on the Destination field indicating it is required

test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit and no redirection occurs; an inline validation error appears on the Departure_City field indicating it is required

test_cases.test_case: Tours: Destination left blank (required field)

steps: 1. Ensure the Tours tab is active
2. Leave the Destination field blank
3. Enter <valid travel date range> in Travel_Date_Range
4. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit and no redirection occurs; an inline validation error appears on the Destination field indicating it is required

test_cases.test_case: Cars: Pick-up location left blank (required field)

steps: 1. Ensure the Cars tab is active
2. Leave the Pick_Up_Location field blank

test_cases.test_case: Hotels: Non-numeric input in numeric field (Adults)
preconditions: User is on the Home page with the Hotels tab selected

4. Enter <valid check-out date> in Check_Out

6. Enter <non-numeric text> in Adults

test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit and no redirection occurs; an inline validation error appears on the Adults field indicating a numeric value is required

test_cases.test_case: Cars: Invalid date/time format in Pick-up Date/Time
preconditions: User is on the Home page with the Cars tab selected
steps: 1. Ensure the Cars tab is active
2. Enter <valid pick-up location> in Pick_Up_Location
3. Enter <valid drop-off location> in Drop_Off_Location
4. Enter <invalid date/time format> in Pick_Up_Date_Time

6. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit and no redirection occurs; an inline validation error appears on the Pick_Up_Date_Time field indicating the entered value is not a valid date/time

test_cases.test_case: Hotels Destination accepts very long free-text input
preconditions: User is on the Home page
steps: 1. Click the Hotels tab
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) in the Destination field
3. Fill all other required Hotels fields with valid values
4. Click Search
test_cases.expected_result: Search succeeds: page redirects to the Hotels Search & Listing page and the search summary visible on that page displays the Destination value preserving the full entered long string (no truncation shown).

test_cases.test_case: Flights Departure City accepts special characters and emoji
preconditions: User is on the Home page
steps: 1. Click the Flights tab
2. Enter a departure city value containing special characters and emoji/unicode in the Departure_City field
3. Fill all other required Flights fields with valid values
4. Click Search
test_cases.expected_result: Search succeeds: page redirects to the Flights Search & Listing page and the search summary visible on that page shows the Departure City value with the special characters/unicode preserved (characters are not replaced with placeholder or removed).

test_cases.test_case: Cars Pick-Up Location with leading/trailing whitespace is normalized
preconditions: User is on the Home page
steps: 1. Click the Cars tab
2. Enter a Pick_Up_Location value that includes leading and trailing whitespace
3. Fill all other required Cars fields with valid values
4. Click Search
test_cases.expected_result: Search succeeds: page redirects to the Cars Search & Listing page and the search summary visible on that page displays the Pick-Up Location value with leading and trailing whitespace trimmed (no visible leading/trailing spaces).

test_cases.test_case: Successful registration with required fields — auto login and redirect to dashboard
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and has access to the Home or Account/Login page
System is configured to automatically log in users on successful registration
steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration form from the Account/Login top navigation
2. Enter <valid first name> in the First Name field
3. Enter <valid last name> in the Last Name field
4. Enter <valid, unique email> in the Email field
5. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
6. Enter <same valid password> in the Confirm Password field
7. Optionally select <mobile country code> and enter <mobile number> in the Mobile Number field
8. Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox
9. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard page is displayed; the top navigation updates to show account-specific menu items (indicating the user is logged in)

test_cases.test_case: Successful registration requiring email verification — verification prompt shown
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and has access to the Home or Account/Login page
System is configured to require email verification before granting dashboard access
steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration form from the Account/Login top navigation
2. Enter <valid first name> in the First Name field
3. Enter <valid last name> in the Last Name field
4. Enter <valid, unique email> in the Email field
5. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
6. Enter <same valid password> in the Confirm Password field
7. Enter <address> in the Address field and select <country> from the Country field (optional)
8. Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox
9. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: An on-screen verification prompt is displayed instructing the user to verify their email; a control to resend the verification email is visible; the top navigation does not show account-specific menu items (indicating the user is not yet logged in)

test_cases.test_case: Registration attempt with an already-registered email shows inline Email field error
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and has access to the Home or Account/Login page
An account already exists using <existing email>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration form from the Account/Login top navigation
2. Enter <valid first name> in the First Name field
3. Enter <valid last name> in the Last Name field
4. Enter <existing email> in the Email field
5. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
6. Enter <same valid password> in the Confirm Password field
7. Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox
8. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed adjacent to the Email field indicating the email is already in use; the registration form remains visible and no redirect to the Dashboard occurs

test_cases.test_case: Submit with ALL required fields empty
preconditions: Visitor is on the Registration page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration page
2. Leave the First Name field blank
3. Leave the Last Name field blank
4. Leave the Email field blank
5. Leave the Password field blank
6. Leave the Confirm Password field blank
7. Leave the Terms and Conditions checkbox unchecked
8. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; inline validation errors appear on First Name, Last Name, Email, Password, Confirm Password and Terms and Conditions fields indicating they are required; no account is created and user remains on the Registration page

test_cases.test_case: Leave a required text field blank (representative: First Name)
preconditions: Visitor is on the Registration page
All other required fields are available to fill
steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration page
2. Leave the First Name field blank
3. Enter <valid last name> in Last Name
4. Enter <valid, unique email> in Email
5. Enter <valid password> in Password
6. Enter the same <valid password> in Confirm Password
7. Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox
8. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the First Name field indicating it is required; form does not submit; account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Leave Email field blank (representative for email type required)
preconditions: Visitor is on the Registration page
All other required fields are filled with valid values
steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration page
2. Enter <valid first name> in First Name
3. Enter <valid last name> in Last Name
4. Leave the Email field blank
5. Enter <valid password> in Password
6. Enter the same <valid password> in Confirm Password
7. Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox
8. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Email field indicating it is required; form does not submit; account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Enter invalid email format
preconditions: Visitor is on the Registration page
All other required fields are filled with valid values
steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration page
2. Enter <valid first name> in First Name
3. Enter <valid last name> in Last Name
4. Enter <invalid email format> in Email
5. Enter <valid password> in Password
6. Enter the same <valid password> in Confirm Password
7. Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox
8. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Email field indicating the email must be in a valid format; form does not submit; account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Register with an Email that already exists (uniqueness constraint)

steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration page

5. Enter <valid password> in Password
6. Enter the same <valid password> in Confirm Password
7. Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox
8. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Email field indicating the email must be unique; form does not submit; account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Password and Confirm Password do not match
preconditions: Visitor is on the Registration page
All other required fields are filled with valid values
steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration page
2. Enter <valid first name> in First Name

4. Enter <valid, unique email> in Email
5. Enter <valid password> in Password
6. Enter a different <non-matching password> in Confirm Password
7. Check the Terms and Conditions checkbox
8. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Confirm Password field indicating it must match the Password; form does not submit; account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Terms and Conditions checkbox left unchecked (required)
preconditions: Visitor is on the Registration page
All other required fields are filled with valid values
steps: 1. Navigate to the Registration page
2. Enter <valid first name> in First Name
3. Enter <valid last name> in Last Name
4. Enter <valid, unique email> in Email
5. Enter <valid password> in Password
6. Enter the same <valid password> in Confirm Password
7. Leave the Terms and Conditions checkbox unchecked
8. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Terms and Conditions checkbox indicating it must be checked to proceed; form does not submit; account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access/submit the Registration form while already authenticated (submit precondition violation)
preconditions: User is already authenticated and has access to the Dashboard
steps: 1. Log in as an authenticated user
2. While authenticated, navigate to the Registration page or click the Register link in navigation
3. Observe the Registration page or UI elements
test_cases.expected_result: Registration is blocked for authenticated users: the Registration form or Register button is not presented (or the user is redirected away from the Registration page); no ability to submit a new registration is available while authenticated

test_cases.test_case: Very long First Name accepted (200+ characters)

steps: 1. Enter a string of 200+ characters in the First_Name field
2. Enter a valid Last_Name
3. Enter a valid Email address
4. Enter a Password
5. Enter the same value in Confirm_Password
6. Check the Terms_and_Conditions checkbox
7. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission succeeds; no inline field errors are displayed for First_Name or other required fields (registration proceeds)

test_cases.test_case: Unicode and emoji characters in name fields are accepted

steps: 1. Enter Unicode and emoji characters in the First_Name field

3. Enter a valid Email address
4. Enter a Password

6. Check the Terms_and_Conditions checkbox

test_cases.test_case: Confirm_Password differs only by a trailing space — mismatch blocks registration

steps: 1. Enter a valid First_Name

3. Enter a valid Email address

6. Check the Terms_and_Conditions checkbox
7. Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Confirm_Password field displays an inline error indicating it does not match Password; registration is blocked (form not submitted)

test_cases.test_case: Press browser Back immediately after successful Register — form should not be pre-filled
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and is on the Registration form
steps: 1. Enter a valid First_Name
2. Enter a valid Last_Name
3. Enter a valid Email address
4. Enter a Password
5. Enter the same value in Confirm_Password
6. Check the Terms_and_Conditions checkbox
7. Click the Register button
8. After the registration flow completes, press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: After pressing Back, the Registration form is shown blank (fields are not pre-filled with prior submission values)

test_cases.test_case: Successful login with email and password (no CAPTCHA)
preconditions: User is not authenticated and has access to the Login page (via Account/Login or redirect from a protected resource)
Valid credentials exist for <user account>
steps: 1. Open the Login page
2. Enter <valid email> in the Email field
3. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
4. (Optional) Check the Remember Me checkbox
5. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: The <Dashboard> page is displayed showing the dashboard heading and account navigation (indicating the user is authenticated)

test_cases.test_case: Successful login when CAPTCHA is required after repeated failures
preconditions: User is not authenticated and has access to the Login page
Multiple consecutive failed login attempts have occurred so CAPTCHA is displayed and required
steps: 1. Open the Login page where the CAPTCHA is visible
2. Enter <valid email> in the Email field
3. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
4. Enter a <valid CAPTCHA response> in the CAPTCHA field
5. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: The <Dashboard> page is displayed showing the dashboard heading and account navigation (indicating the user is authenticated)

test_cases.test_case: Open Forgot Password flow from Login page
preconditions: User is not authenticated and has access to the Login page
steps: 1. Open the Login page
2. Click the Forgot Password? link
test_cases.expected_result: The Password Reset page is displayed with its heading and an input field for the user's email address

test_cases.test_case: Sign in with Google when social logins are enabled

Social logins are enabled and the Sign in with Google button is visible on the Login page
steps: 1. Open the Login page
2. Verify the Sign in with Google button is visible
3. Click the Sign in with Google button
test_cases.expected_result: The <Dashboard> page is displayed showing the dashboard heading and account navigation (indicating the user is authenticated after completing the social sign-in flow)

test_cases.test_case: Sign in with Facebook when social logins are enabled
preconditions: User is not authenticated and has access to the Login page
Social logins are enabled and the Sign in with Facebook button is visible on the Login page
steps: 1. Open the Login page
2. Verify the Sign in with Facebook button is visible
3. Click the Sign in with Facebook button
test_cases.expected_result: The <Dashboard> page is displayed showing the dashboard heading and account navigation (indicating the user is authenticated after completing the social sign-in flow)

test_cases.test_case: Invalid credentials show error and clear Password field
preconditions: User is not authenticated and has access to the Login page
steps: 1. Open the Login page
2. Enter <invalid email or valid email with wrong password> in the Email field
3. Enter <invalid password> in the Password field
4. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed indicating the credentials are invalid; the Password field is cleared and ready for re-entry

test_cases.test_case: Submit with invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Enter <invalid email format> into the Email field
3. Enter <valid password> into the Password field
4. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Email field displays an inline validation error indicating the email format is invalid; the form does not submit and the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Login attempt with incorrect credentials
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Enter <registered email> into the Email field
3. Enter <incorrect password> into the Password field
4. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login fails; the page shows an authentication failure error message and the Password field is cleared; the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to submit when CAPTCHA is required but left blank
preconditions: User has made multiple consecutive failed login attempts so that CAPTCHA is required
User is on the Login page and the Captcha field is visible
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page (Captcha is visible due to prior failed attempts)
2. Enter <registered email> into the Email field
3. Enter <correct password> into the Password field
4. Leave the Captcha field blank
5. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Captcha field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; Login is blocked; the form does not submit and the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Social login buttons should not appear when social logins are disabled
preconditions: Social logins are disabled in system settings
User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Observe the presence of social login buttons
test_cases.expected_result: Sign in with Google and Sign in with Facebook buttons are not visible on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated user cannot perform Login action (precondition not met)
preconditions: User is already authenticated and has an active session
steps: 1. While authenticated, navigate to Account / Login or the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Login action is not available: the Login button/form is not visible (the Login action precondition prevents offering the Login action); user is not presented the login form

test_cases.test_case: CAPTCHA becomes visible after repeated failed attempts (timing check)
preconditions: User has made multiple consecutive failed login attempts
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page after the repeated failed attempts
test_cases.expected_result: Captcha field is visible on the Login page before any new user input; the Captcha field is displayed and marked required

test_cases.test_case: Login with email that has leading and trailing whitespace
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Login page
A valid account exists for the canonical email (without surrounding whitespace) and known password
steps: 1. Enter the canonical email with leading and trailing whitespace characters into the Email field
2. Enter the correct password into the Password field
3. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: the user is redirected to the Dashboard or the previously requested page (Dashboard page is visible).

test_cases.test_case: Attempt login using an Email value containing emoji / unusual Unicode characters
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter an Email value that includes emoji or unusual Unicode characters into the Email field
2. Enter any value into the Password field
3. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Is blocked / error shown: the Email field displays an inline validation error preventing submission; no redirect occurs and the Login form remains visible.

test_cases.test_case: CAPTCHA appears after repeated failed attempts; submitting valid credentials without completing CAPTCHA is blocked
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Login page
CAPTCHA is configured to become visible/required after multiple consecutive failed attempts (visible_when: 'multiple consecutive failed attempts')
steps: 1. Enter invalid credentials and click the Login button
2. Repeat step 1 until the CAPTCHA control becomes visible on the Login form
3. Enter valid Email and Password for an existing account, leaving the CAPTCHA control empty or unanswered
4. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Is blocked / error shown: submission is blocked; the CAPTCHA field displays a required validation indication and the Password field is cleared; no redirect to Dashboard occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Login with valid credentials (prevent duplicate submit)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the Login page
A valid account exists for the provided Email and Password
steps: 1. Enter a valid Email into the Email field
2. Enter the correct Password into the Password field
3. Rapidly click the Login button twice in immediate succession
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: the user is redirected once to the Dashboard or previously requested page (Dashboard page is visible); only one navigation to the Dashboard occurs and no duplicate submission UI state or second redirect is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Request password reset with registered email shows confirmation

2. Enter <registered email> in the Email field

test_cases.test_case: Change password using a valid reset link redirects to Login with success message

test_cases.test_case: Submit Forgot Password with Email left blank

Navigate to the Forgot Password page
steps: 1. Ensure the Email field is empty
2. Click the Reset Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Email field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; no reset confirmation message is shown and the Forgot Password form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Submit Forgot Password with invalid email format
preconditions: User is unauthenticated
Navigate to the Forgot Password page
steps: 1. Enter <invalid email format> into the Email field
2. Click the Reset Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Email field displays an inline validation error indicating the value is not a valid email format; the form does not submit; no reset confirmation message is shown

test_cases.test_case: Submit Forgot Password with an email not found in the system

Navigate to the Forgot Password page

test_cases.expected_result: The page shows an error indicating the entered email address is not found and the Forgot Password form remains editable; no reset confirmation message is shown and no reset link is sent

test_cases.test_case: Password reset page: required New Password left blank (dialog required-field representative)

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Change Password with an expired reset link

steps: 1. Enter <valid new password> in the New_Password field
2. Enter the same <valid new password> in the Confirm_Password field
3. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: An error is shown indicating the reset link is invalid or expired; the password is not changed; the user is not redirected to the login page and remains on the Password Reset page with the form editable

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated user cannot use Forgot Password (precondition not met)
preconditions: User is authenticated and on their account/dashboard
steps: 1. While authenticated, navigate to the Forgot Password page or the Account/Login path

)

+ trailing whitespace)

steps: 1. Navigate to the Flights Search page
2. Enter <valid Trip_Type> in the Trip_Type field
3. Enter <valid Departure_City> in the Departure_City field
4. Enter <valid Arrival_City> in the Arrival_City field
5. Enter <valid Departure_Date> in the Departure_Date field
6. Enter <non-numeric input> in the Adults field
7. Select <valid Cabin_Class> in the Cabin_Class field
8. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit or navigate to results; Adults field displays an inline validation error indicating the value must be numeric; user remains on the search form

test_cases.test_case: Submit Search with zero passengers (Adults=0, Children=0, Infants=0) violating 'valid flight search inputs' precondition
preconditions: Visitor is on the Flights Search page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Flights Search page
2. Enter <valid Trip_Type> in the Trip_Type field
3. Enter <valid Departure_City> in the Departure_City field
4. Enter <valid Arrival_City> in the Arrival_City field
5. Enter <valid Departure_Date> in the Departure_Date field
6. Enter 0 in the Adults field
7. Enter 0 in the Children field
8. Enter 0 in the Infants field
9. Select <valid Cabin_Class> in the Cabin_Class field
10. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit or navigate to results; an inline validation error appears indicating at least one passenger must be selected (error shown on passenger count fields); user remains on the search form

test_cases.test_case: Very long string entered into Departure_City is rejected at the boundary of display/length limits
preconditions: Visitor is on the Flights Search form page
All other required search fields are available (Trip_Type, Arrival_City, Departure_Date, Adults, Cabin_Class)
steps: 1. Select Trip_Type = One-way
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) in the Departure_City field
3. Enter a valid Arrival_City value
4. Enter a valid Departure_Date
5. Enter Adults = 1
6. Select Cabin_Class = Economy
7. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Search is blocked; the Departure_City field displays a visible inline error indicating the value exceeds the allowed length (no redirect to the Listing page)

test_cases.test_case: Unicode / emoji / special characters entered into Arrival_City are preserved and shown in the search summary
preconditions: Visitor is on the Flights Search form page
All other required search fields are available (Trip_Type, Departure_City, Departure_Date, Adults, Cabin_Class)
steps: 1. Select Trip_Type = One-way
2. Enter a valid Departure_City value
3. Enter a value containing Unicode characters and emoji in the Arrival_City field
4. Enter a valid Departure_Date
5. Enter Adults = 1
6. Select Cabin_Class = Economy
7. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; user is redirected to the Listing page and the search summary/header visibly displays the Arrival_City value including the entered Unicode/special characters

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in city fields is trimmed before display after search
preconditions: Visitor is on the Flights Search form page
All other required search fields are available (Trip_Type, Departure_Date, Adults, Cabin_Class)
steps: 1. Select Trip_Type = One-way
2. Enter a Departure_City value with leading and trailing spaces
3. Enter an Arrival_City value with leading and trailing spaces
4. Enter a valid Departure_Date
5. Enter Adults = 1
6. Select Cabin_Class = Economy
7. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; user is redirected to the Listing page and the search summary/header shows Departure_City and Arrival_City values with leading/trailing whitespace removed (trimmed) in the visible text

test_cases.test_case: Fill required details for single traveler and continue to Payment
preconditions: User is on the Flight Booking page; a specific flight itinerary and passenger count have been selected (user may be logged in or proceeding as guest)
steps: 1. In Traveler 1, select <Title> from the Title dropdown
2. Enter <First Name> in the First Name field for Traveler 1
3. Enter <Last Name> in the Last Name field for Traveler 1
4. Enter <Date of Birth> in the Date of Birth field for Traveler 1
5. Enter <Passport Number> in the Passport Number field and <Passport Expiry> in the Passport Expiry field for Traveler 1
6. Enter <valid email> in Lead Contact Email and <valid phone> in Lead Contact Phone
7. Click Continue
test_cases.expected_result: The Payment Processing page opens showing the booking summary (itinerary and the passenger name for Traveler 1) and available payment options

test_cases.test_case: Add a second traveler and verify fare breakdown updates to show two passengers
preconditions: User is on the Flight Booking page; a specific flight itinerary and an initial passenger count have been selected (user may be logged in or proceeding as guest)
steps: 1. Click Add Traveler to add Traveler 2 (new repeating traveler row)
2. In Traveler 1, select <Title> and enter <First Name>, <Last Name>, <Date of Birth>, <Passport Number>, and <Passport Expiry>
3. In Traveler 2, select <Title> and enter <First Name>, <Last Name>, <Date of Birth>, <Passport Number>, and <Passport Expiry>
4. Observe the Fare Breakdown section
test_cases.expected_result: Fare breakdown displays two per-passenger fare rows (one for each traveler) and an Overall Total is shown reflecting both passengers

test_cases.test_case: Complete required details for two travelers and continue to Payment
preconditions: User is on the Flight Booking page; a specific flight itinerary and passenger count have been selected (user may be logged in or proceeding as guest)
steps: 1. Click Add Traveler to add Traveler 2
2. Fill required fields for Traveler 1: select <Title>, enter <First Name>, <Last Name>, <Date of Birth>, <Passport Number>, and <Passport Expiry>
3. Fill required fields for Traveler 2: select <Title>, enter <First Name>, <Last Name>, <Date of Birth>, <Passport Number>, and <Passport Expiry>
4. Enter <valid email> in Lead Contact Email and <valid phone> in Lead Contact Phone
5. Click Continue
test_cases.expected_result: The Payment Processing page opens showing the booking summary that lists both travelers and the available payment options

test_cases.test_case: Set optional meal preference and seat selection for a traveler and verify they are shown in the traveler summary
preconditions: User is on the Flight Booking page; a specific flight itinerary and passenger count have been selected (user may be logged in or proceeding as guest)
steps: 1. Expand Traveler 1 details to edit passenger optional fields
2. Enter <meal preference> in Meal Preferences for Traveler 1 and select <seat selection> in Seat Selection for Traveler 1
3. Collapse or close Traveler 1 details to view the traveler summary row
test_cases.expected_result: Traveler 1 summary displays the selected Meal: <meal preference> and Seat: <seat selection>

test_cases.test_case: Submit with all required fields empty
preconditions: Booking flow has advanced from Flights Search & Listing with a specific flight itinerary and passenger count selected
User is on the Flight Booking page
steps: 1. Navigate to the Flight Booking page with a selected itinerary and passenger count
2. For each traveler item, leave Title, First Name, Last Name, Date of Birth, Passport Number and Passport Expiry blank
3. Leave Lead Contact Email and Phone blank
4. Click the Continue button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; inline validation errors appear on each required field: Title, First Name, Last Name, Date of Birth, Passport Number, Passport Expiry, Email and Phone indicating they are required; user remains on the Flight Booking page and is not navigated to Payment Processing

test_cases.test_case: Leave a required text field blank (representative: Traveler First Name)

test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; an inline validation error appears on the First Name field indicating it is required; user remains on the Flight Booking page and is not navigated to Payment Processing

test_cases.test_case: Leave required dropdown blank (Title) for a traveler

3. Fill all other required traveler and lead contact fields with valid values (First Name, Last Name, Date of Birth, Passport Number, Passport Expiry, Lead Contact Email, Lead Contact Phone)
4. Click the Continue button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; an inline validation error appears on the Title field indicating a selection is required; user remains on the Flight Booking page and is not navigated to Payment Processing

test_cases.test_case: Enter invalid format in Lead Contact Email

test_cases.test_case: Enter invalid date in a required date field (Date of Birth)

steps: 1. Open the Flight Booking page with a selected itinerary and passenger count

test_cases.expected_result: Continue is blocked; the page shows an error or page-level indication that a flight itinerary and passenger count must be selected before proceeding (the booking cannot progress); user remains on the Flight Booking page and is not navigated to Payment Processing

steps: 1. Select Title = Ms for Traveler 1

test_cases.test_case: Double-click Continue immediately after filling valid required fields

test_cases.test_case: Submit Tours search form and open listing page
preconditions: User is a Visitor (guest or authenticated)
Visitor is in the Tours section with valid search criteria entered (destination and travel dates)
steps: 1. On the Tours Search form, verify the search criteria are present (destination and travel dates)
2. Click the Search button on the Tours Search form
test_cases.expected_result: Tours Listing page is displayed showing tour result cards for matching criteria; each visible card shows an image, tour name, destination, duration, starting price per person, availability status, and traveler rating

test_cases.test_case: Sort listing by a chosen criterion
preconditions: User is a Visitor (guest or authenticated)
Visitor is in the Tours section with valid search criteria entered (destination and travel dates)
steps: 1. Click the Search button on the Tours Search form to open the Tours Listing page
2. Select <sort criterion> from the Sort By dropdown (options: popularity, price, duration, rating)
3. Wait for the listing to refresh
test_cases.expected_result: Tours Listing displays result cards resorted by the selected <sort criterion>; the ordering of cards updates to reflect sorting by the chosen criterion

test_cases.test_case: Apply multiple sidebar filters and refine results
preconditions: User is a Visitor (guest or authenticated)
Visitor is in the Tours section with valid search criteria entered (destination and travel dates)
steps: 1. Click the Search button on the Tours Search form to open the Tours Listing page

2. In the Sidebar Filters panel, set Destination to <destination>, set Tour Type to <tour type>, set Price Range to <price range>, set Duration to <duration>, and set Departure Start/End Dates to <departure start date> and <departure end date>
3. Click Apply Filters (or wait for filters to apply) and wait for the listing to update
test_cases.expected_result: Tours Listing shows only tour result cards matching the applied filters (destination, tour type, price range, duration, departure dates); unrelated tour cards are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Open Tour Details from a result card
preconditions: User is a Visitor (guest or authenticated)
Visitor is in the Tours section with valid search criteria entered (destination and travel dates)
steps: 1. Click the Search button on the Tours Search form to open the Tours Listing page
2. Click the View Details link on a selected tour result card
test_cases.expected_result: The Tour Details page for <selected tour> opens, showing the tour title, main image, starting price, and a booking call-to-action visible on the page

test_cases.test_case: Initiate booking from a tour result card
preconditions: User is a Visitor (guest or authenticated)
Visitor is in the Tours section with valid search criteria entered (destination and travel dates)
steps: 1. Click the Search button on the Tours Search form to open the Tours Listing page
2. Click the Book button on a selected tour result card
test_cases.expected_result: Booking page for <selected tour> opens, showing the booking form with available dates and starting price per person

test_cases.test_case: Verify tour result cards display required fields
preconditions: User is a Visitor (guest or authenticated)
Visitor is in the Tours section with valid search criteria entered (destination and travel dates)
steps: 1. Click the Search button on the Tours Search form to open the Tours Listing page
2. Observe at least one tour result card in the listing
test_cases.expected_result: At least one tour result card is visible and displays an image, tour name, destination, duration, starting price per person, availability status, and traveler rating

test_cases.test_case: Submit search with Destination left blank (required text field)
preconditions: Visitor is on the Tours Search form
steps: 1. Navigate to the Tours Search form
2. Leave the Destination field blank
3. Enter <valid start date> in Start_Date
4. Enter <valid end date> in End_Date
5. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Destination field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; user remains on the search form (no redirect to Tours Listing page)

test_cases.test_case: Submit search with all required fields empty
preconditions: Visitor is on the Tours Search form
steps: 1. Navigate to the Tours Search form
2. Leave the Destination field blank
3. Leave the Start_Date field blank
4. Leave the End_Date field blank
5. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on Destination, Start_Date, and End_Date indicating they are required; the form does not submit; user remains on the search form (no redirect to Tours Listing page)

test_cases.test_case: Submit search with Start Date left blank (required date field)
preconditions: Visitor is on the Tours Search form
steps: 1. Navigate to the Tours Search form
2. Enter <valid destination> in Destination
3. Leave the Start_Date field blank
4. Enter <valid end date> in End_Date
5. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Start_Date field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; user remains on the search form (no redirect to Tours Listing page)

test_cases.test_case: Submit search with invalid date format in Start Date
preconditions: Visitor is on the Tours Search form
steps: 1. Navigate to the Tours Search form
2. Enter <valid destination> in Destination
3. Enter <invalid date format> in Start_Date
4. Enter <valid end date> in End_Date
5. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Start_Date field displays an inline validation error indicating the date format is invalid; the form does not submit; user remains on the search form (no redirect to Tours Listing page)

test_cases.test_case: Submit search with End Date earlier than Start Date (date ordering constraint)
preconditions: Visitor is on the Tours Search form
steps: 1. Navigate to the Tours Search form
2. Enter <valid destination> in Destination
3. Enter <start date> in Start_Date
4. Enter <end date earlier than start date> in End_Date
5. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the End_Date field indicating it must be after the Start_Date; the form does not submit; user remains on the search form (no redirect to Tours Listing page)

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Tours search when precondition 'in Tours section' is not met
preconditions: Visitor is NOT in the Tours section (e.g., on Home page search widget with a non-Tours tab active or on another site section)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home page search widget or another non-Tours section
2. Ensure the Tours tab is not selected
3. Enter <valid destination> and <valid travel dates> into the visible search widget fields
4. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Search is not performed for Tours; user remains on the current section (no redirect to Tours Listing page); a visible inline or page-level indicator appears stating the Tours search cannot be executed because the user is not in the Tours section or has not selected the Tours search

test_cases.test_case: Apply sidebar filters with Departure End Date before Departure Start Date
preconditions: Visitor is on the Tours Listing page with results visible
steps: 1. Navigate to the Tours Listing page with results displayed
2. Open the Sidebar Filters panel
3. Enter <departure start date> in Departure_Start_Date
4. Enter <departure end date earlier than start date> in Departure_End_Date
5. Click the Apply/Filter button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Departure_End_Date field indicating it must be after Departure_Start_Date; filters are not applied; the listing remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Very long Destination string accepted and shown on listing

Valid Start_Date and End_Date already entered
steps: 1. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the Destination field
2. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Search action succeeds; user is redirected to the Tours Listing page and the search summary on the listing page displays the entered Destination string (system accepted the long input) — no validation error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Destination containing special characters and emoji is preserved

Valid Start_Date and End_Date already entered
steps: 1. Enter a Destination value composed primarily of special characters and emoji into the Destination field

test_cases.expected_result: Search action succeeds; user is redirected to the Tours Listing page and the search summary displays the Destination with the special characters and emoji preserved (no inline error shown)

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Destination is trimmed on submit
preconditions: Tours Search Form is open

steps: 1. Enter a Destination value with leading and trailing whitespace in the Destination field

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Search button does not create duplicate searches

All required search fields populated with valid values
steps: 1. Click the Search button twice in rapid succession (double-click)
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked/ignored; user is redirected only once to the Tours Listing page and the listing displays a single set of results / single search summary (no duplicate result pages or duplicate visible search runs)

test_cases.test_case: Browser Back after successful search restores search form with previous criteria
preconditions: Tours Search Form is open

module_context.summary: Provides the site's primary search widget allowing visitors to enter service-specific search criteria for Hotels, Flights, Tours, or Cars and initiate searches that lead to result listings.

module_context.summary: Collects personal and contact information to create a new user account with validation and optional email verification.

The main navigation remains available while contextual menus update after successful registration and login.

module_context.summary: A page that authenticates users via email/password (with optional social logins), handles remember-me and CAPTCHA on repeated failures, and redirects authenticated users to their dashboard or previously requested page.

module_context.summary: Allows an unauthenticated user to request a password reset by submitting their account email so a time-limited reset link is sent.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation's Account/Login path, this module is part of the authentication flow tied to Login/Signup and precedes the Password Reset page reached via the emailed link; it sits outside booking flows and is used to regain account access before reaching the User Dashboard or booking management.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Unauthenticated user attempting account recovery who knows (or enters) the registered email address.

module_context.summary: Collects hotel search criteria and presents a filterable, sortable listing of hotel result cards showing name, location, rating, thumbnail, starting price, amenities, and a Book Now action.

module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Search parameters entered (destination, check-in/check-out dates, number of rooms, and guest counts) and the user is in the Hotels search flow (guest or authenticated) with currency/language set.

module_context.summary: Displays comprehensive hotel information (photos, description, map), room options with pricing and policies, guest reviews, and a booking form that captures guest details and price breakdown prior to payment.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the main navigation via Home -> Hotels or Hotels Search & Listing, this module is the detailed property view users reach after searching for hotels and selecting a result; it precedes Payment Processing and Booking Management in the booking lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User has performed a hotel search and selected a specific hotel and stay dates (guest count and room selection available); booking completion requires an authenticated (logged-in) user.

module_context.summary: Collects flight search criteria and displays a sortable, filterable list of matching flight options with fares, schedules, stops, and expandable details like baggage and fare rules.

It connects user-provided search criteria to selectable flight results and passes the chosen option into the booking/payment lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Visitor is on the site (logged in or guest) and has valid flight search inputs provided (trip type, origin/destination, dates, passenger counts, cabin class).

module_context.summary: Displays the selected flight itinerary, passenger and cabin details with a fare breakdown, and collects validated traveler and lead contact information required for completing the booking.

module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: A specific flight itinerary and passenger count have been selected (user may be logged in or proceeding as guest) and the booking flow has advanced from Flights Search & Listing.

module_context.summary: Provides search and listing of available tours with filters, sorting, and result cards that display images, destination, duration, starting price, availability, and traveler ratings.

It serves as the discovery layer that narrows options and presents selectable tour results for booking or deeper inspection.

module_context.summary: Presents full tour information (images, day-by-day itinerary, inclusions/exclusions, departure dates, per-person pricing, map, reviews, and terms) and captures traveler and contact details to calculate the total cost and create a booking.

module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: A specific tour has been selected from Tours Search & Listing; if not authenticated, the user will be redirected to Login before completing a booking.

module_context.summary: Provides car rental search and dynamic listing of available vehicles by category with detailed specs, pricing, and sidebar filters.

It sits alongside other transport modules (Flights, Tours) and links into Booking Management and User Dashboard for completed reservations.

module_context.summary: Displays the selected vehicle, rental period, pick-up/drop-off details, pricing breakdown, driver information form, optional add-ons and insurance selection, and a terms acceptance step before proceeding to payment.

It is the confirmation and data-collection step where selected vehicle and rental options are finalized before payment.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: A specific car has been chosen from Cars Search & Listing and rental dates, pick-up/drop-off locations and times are specified; user is on the booking page ready to provide driver and contact details.

module_context.summary: Provides country-specific visa requirements and a visa application workflow including form entry, document uploads, fee details, and status tracking.

module_context.summary: A personalized account area that aggregates bookings, profile details, wallet/credits, wishlist, reviews, settings, and logout functionality.

module_context.summary: Shows detailed information for an individual booking including confirmation number, service and traveler details, payment information, current status, and available modify or cancel actions when allowed.
